fields.narrative_intro: *A gobsmacked person and a smarmy person discuss Karpathy joining Anthropic on a runaway train.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "Karpathy just joined Anthropic's pre-training team."
SMARMY PERSON: "Cool. Who's Karpathy?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "OpenAI co-founder. Tesla AI director. Wrote half the internet's neural net tutorials."
SMARMY PERSON: "Still not ringing a bell."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "He's literally building a team where Claude trains Claude."
SMARMY PERSON: "Oh — the guy OpenAI lost."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The gobsmacked person's jaw unhinges completely. The smarmy person scrolls a phone. Outside the train window, a blurred sign reads 'FRONTIER: NEXT FEW YEARS' as the train accelerates downhill.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
SMARMY PERSON: "So Claude is now doing Karpathy's pretraining research?"
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "Starting immediately, yes."
SMARMY PERSON: "Claude replaced Karpathy faster than Karpathy replaced OpenAI."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A small Claude logo sits in the conductor's seat at the front of the runaway train, confidently gripping the throttle.]

narrative_summary: The joke escalates from 'who's Karpathy' ignorance to the ironic punchline that Claude is already automating the job Karpathy just arrived to do, capped by Claude literally driving the runaway train.
rationale: Candidate 4 earns its punchline structurally — the 'who's Karpathy' ignorance setup pays off with the genuinely surprising reversal that Claude replaced Karpathy faster than Karpathy replaced OpenAI, which is a tight, earned joke rather than a restatement. The escalation from feigned ignorance to dawning horror also makes the best use of the 6-panel template's build mechanics.
